blurb: Affliction (SL/SL) Warlock is the most notorious attrition spec in TBC arena — Siphon Life and Soul Link make you nearly unkillable while dots and Unstable Affliction dismantle healers' mana and patience. You don't have a burst window; you ARE the win condition, one tick at a time.
statPriorityRationale: Affliction pressure is inevitable rather than bursty, so the entire gear plan is outlasting the enemy's attempts to delete you — TBC warlocks famously stack stamina above all, and the arena data confirms it: SL/SL survivability turns every game into a timer you win.

faq.question: What rating are these Affliction Warlocks?
faq.answer: This snapshot covers 4 Affliction Warlocks between 2108 and 2922 rating (average 2449) on the TBC Classic arena ladder, refreshed from public leaderboard data.

faq.question: What is the most-used Affliction Warlock PvP gear right now?
faq.answer: The highest-consensus picks are Merciless Gladiator's Dreadweave Mantle (100%), Sergeant's Heavy Cape (100%), Veteran's Dreadweave Cuffs (100%). Anything above ~70% usage at this rating range is effectively mandatory.

faq.question: How much of the Affliction Warlock BiS list is PvP gear vs raid gear?
faq.answer: 93% of the most-used slots are arena/honor items — for Affliction Warlocks the resilience on PvP gear beats raid alternatives almost everywhere. The exceptions are listed as alternatives per slot.

faq.question: What weapon do Affliction Warlocks use in arena?
faq.answer: Merciless Gladiator's Spellblade is the main-hand of choice (50% of surveyed players).

faq.question: What gems do Affliction Warlocks socket for PvP?
faq.answer: Across all sockets, the most-used gems in this snapshot are +10 Spell Penetration, +4 Resilience Rating and +6 Stamina, +18 Stamina & 5% Stun Resist. Socket bonuses are usually ignored unless they align with these.
